label: PASS
rationale: The command spans GlobalRange and Locking_Range1, but Level 0 reports RangeCrossingBehavior 0 and the configured range is write-unlocked, so Opal 4.3.7 allows the device to process the transfer when all addressed ranges are unlocked.

label: FAIL
rationale: The write spans multiple ranges with RangeCrossingBehavior 0 and all addressed ranges are write-unlocked. Opal 4.3.7 says the device processes the transfer in that condition, so terminating it as INVALID_PARAMETER is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The write crosses more than one Locking range while Level 0 reports RangeCrossingBehavior 1. Opal 4.3.7 requires such a range-crossing command to be terminated, even though the ranges are unlocked, so SUCCESS is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The write to LBA 10 through 12 is wholly inside Locking_Range1, so the range-crossing bit does not apply. That range is write-unlocked, and Core 5.7.3.2 permits writing user data in that state.

label: FAIL
rationale: The write to LBA 10 through 12 stays within a single unlocked Locking range, so RangeCrossingBehavior 1 is irrelevant. Core 5.7.3.2 permits a write when WriteLockEnabled is true and WriteLocked is false, so INVALID_PARAMETER is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The read spans GlobalRange and Locking_Range1, Level 0 reports RangeCrossingBehavior 0, and the configured range is read-unlocked. Opal 4.3.7 allows processing a read spanning multiple unlocked ranges in that condition.

label: FAIL
rationale: The read crosses multiple ranges with RangeCrossingBehavior 0 and no addressed range is read-locked. Opal 4.3.7 says to process the transfer when all crossed ranges are unlocked, so INVALID_PARAMETER is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The read spans multiple Locking ranges while Level 0 reports RangeCrossingBehavior 1. Opal 4.3.7 requires the device to terminate such a command, so SUCCESS is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The read crosses multiple Locking ranges and Level 0 reports RangeCrossingBehavior 1. Opal 4.3.7 requires termination rather than returning user data, so returning Pattern EE is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Although RangeCrossingBehavior is 0, the addressed read crosses into Locking_Range1 with ReadLocked true. The range-crossing bit does not authorize access to locked ranges, and Core 5.7.2.2.8/5.7.3.2 prohibit normal read data from a read-locked range, so returning Pattern FF is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Although RangeCrossingBehavior is 0, the addressed write crosses into Locking_Range1 with WriteLocked true. The crossing rule only permits processing when all addressed ranges are unlocked, and Core 5.7.2.2.9/5.7.3.2 prohibit normal writes to a write-locked range, so SUCCESS is not compliant.
